entity: University of Nevada Oral History Program
explicit_text: Since 1965, the University of Nevada Oral History Program has been collecting firsthand accounts of Nevada's past. I participated in their statewide mining oral history project in 1999 at my home in Carson City. They helped preserve my stories about mining, politics, and life in Nevada throughout the 1900s. Their work ensures that memories like mine are accessible for research and education.
implicit_text: Since 1965, a local oral history initiative at a major Nevada university has been collecting firsthand accounts of the state's past. I participated in their statewide mining oral history project in 1999 at my home in Carson City. They helped preserve my stories about mining, politics, and life in Nevada throughout the 1900s. Their work ensures that memories like mine are accessible for research and education.

explicit_text: I was born in Italy on April 27, 1910, near the Reno River, close to Bologna. My family owned much land there, but my father left soon after I was born. I lived in Italy until I was five before moving to the United States. Speaking Italian and its many dialects came naturally to me, especially since I grew up around Italian miners and spoke their languages fluently.
implicit_text: I was born in a European country on April 27, 1910, near the Reno River, close to Bologna. My family owned much land there, but my father left soon after I was born. I lived there until I was five before moving to the United States. Speaking Italian and its many dialects came naturally to me, especially since I grew up around Italian miners and spoke their languages fluently.

explicit_text: I first came to the United States at age five in 1915, settling with my mother and stepfather in Utah. Growing up here, I worked in various industries like sheep herding, ranching, and mining across Nevada. The U.S. became my home, and I embraced its diverse cultures, especially among immigrant mining communities. My life story is deeply intertwined with the American West during the 20th century.
implicit_text: I first came to this country at age five in 1915, settling with my mother and stepfather in Utah. Growing up here, I worked in various industries like sheep herding, ranching, and mining across Nevada. This nation became my home, and I embraced its diverse cultures, especially among immigrant mining communities. My life story is deeply intertwined with the American West during the 20th century.

explicit_text: After arriving in the U.S., I lived in Utah with my mother and stepfather. We moved frequently among mining camps, and I attended school in Bingham during World War I. It was there I learned several languages from the diverse immigrant population. Utah was where I first worked in sheep camps and ranches before heading to Nevada to mine.
implicit_text: After arriving in the U.S., I lived in a western state with my mother and stepfather. We moved frequently among mining camps, and I attended school in Bingham during World War I. It was there I learned several languages from the diverse immigrant population. That state was where I first worked in sheep camps and ranches before heading to Nevada to mine.

explicit_text: Pioche, Nevada, was where I started working underground in the Bristol mine, even though I was underage. It was a tough mining town with many colorful characters, and I became known as a prizefighter there. My experiences in Pioche shaped much of my early life and gave me a deep connection to Nevada's mining culture.
implicit_text: A small mining town in southern Nevada was where I started working underground in the Bristol mine, even though I was underage. It was a tough mining town with many colorful characters, and I became known as a prizefighter there. My experiences in that town shaped much of my early life and gave me a deep connection to Nevada's mining culture.

explicit_text: Nevada has been the backdrop for most of my life and work throughout the 1900s. From mining and prizefighting to serving as a deputy mine inspector and eventually warden of the Nevada State Prison, I experienced many facets of this state. I developed friendships with political figures like Governor Charlie Russell and enjoyed hunting and fishing across Nevada's landscapes. My story is a reflection of Nevada's rich history and challenges.
implicit_text: This western state has been the backdrop for most of my life and work throughout the 1900s. From mining and prizefighting to serving as a deputy mine inspector and eventually warden of the state prison, I experienced many facets of this place. I developed friendships with political figures like Governor Charlie Russell and enjoyed hunting and fishing across its landscapes. My story is a reflection of this state's rich history and challenges.

explicit_text: I lived in Carson City later in life, and it was in my ranch-style home there that I gave my oral history interview in August 1999. Carson City was a comfortable place where I spent many afternoons sharing stories about Nevada's mining past and political life. It became my home base for reflecting on nearly nine decades of experiences.
implicit_text: I lived in the capital city of Nevada later in life, and it was in my ranch-style home there that I gave my oral history interview in August 1999. That city was a comfortable place where I spent many afternoons sharing stories about Nevada's mining past and political life. It became my home base for reflecting on nearly nine decades of experiences.

explicit_text: My family’s land in Italy was located about fifty miles from Bologna, near the Reno River. Though I was born in Italy, I was not very close to my extended family there. Bologna represents the region where my paternal family lived before I moved to the United States as a child.
implicit_text: My family’s land in Italy was located about fifty miles from a major city in the Emilia-Romagna region, near the Reno River. Though I was born in Italy, I was not very close to my extended family there. That city represents the region where my paternal family lived before I moved to the United States as a child.

explicit_text: The Reno River area in Italy was where my family owned much land, and it was near this river that my father’s family lived. Though I was born there, I left Italy at age five. The Reno River region is part of my early roots before coming to America.
implicit_text: The Reno River area in Italy was where my family owned much land, and it was near this river that my father’s family lived. Though I was born there, I left Italy at age five. The Reno River region is part of my early roots before coming to America.

explicit_text: My parents were married in Delzell, Illinois, and after leaving Italy, my mother returned there with me. It was in Delzell that my mother divorced my father. Though I was born in Italy, Delzell was an important place in my family’s American story before we moved west.
implicit_text: My parents were married in a small town in Illinois, and after leaving Italy, my mother returned there with me. It was in that town that my mother divorced my father. Though I was born in Italy, this Illinois town was an important place in my family’s American story before we moved west.

explicit_text: In Utah, my stepfather worked as a mechanic and got a job at a garage that sold Hupmobiles and Model T Fords. This was during the early days of automobiles, and he was quite skilled with cars. The Hupmobile agency was part of our life in Ephraim, Utah, where we lived among mostly Mormon families.
implicit_text: In Utah, my stepfather worked as a mechanic and got a job at a garage that sold early automobiles including a lesser-known brand and the Model T Ford. This was during the early days of automobiles, and he was quite skilled with cars. The garage was part of our life in Ephraim, Utah, where we lived among mostly Mormon families.

explicit_text: Alongside the Hupmobile, the garage where my stepfather worked also sold Model T Fords. These cars were becoming popular during our time in Utah, and my stepfather’s mechanical skills extended to them. The Model T Ford was part of the automotive landscape in Ephraim where I grew up.
implicit_text: Alongside the lesser-known automobile brand, the garage where my stepfather worked also sold Model T Fords. These cars were becoming popular during our time in Utah, and my stepfather’s mechanical skills extended to them. The Model T Ford was part of the automotive landscape in Ephraim where I grew up.

explicit_text: During World War I, I was attending school in Bingham, Utah, where many of the kids were immigrants from Serbia and Austria. I learned several languages from these communities during that time. The war years shaped the multicultural environment I grew up in and influenced my language skills.
implicit_text: During the First World War, I was attending school in Bingham, Utah, where many of the kids were immigrants from Serbia and Austria. I learned several languages from these communities during that time. The war years shaped the multicultural environment I grew up in and influenced my language skills.

explicit_text: Chris Larson was a rancher in Sanpete County, Utah, who owned thousands of acres but lacked enough water for irrigation. In 1920, my stepfather got a contract to drill an irrigation tunnel for him through a mountain to bring water to his land. Larson was also a mechanic interested in automobiles, and my stepfather worked for him during that project.
implicit_text: Chris Larson was a rancher in Sanpete County, Utah, who owned thousands of acres but lacked enough water for irrigation. In 1920, my stepfather got a contract to drill an irrigation tunnel for him through a mountain to bring water to his land. Larson was also a mechanic interested in automobiles, and my stepfather worked for him during that project.

explicit_text: Charlie Russell was a friend of mine who became governor of Nevada. When he was elected, he appointed me as warden of the Nevada State Prison. Although it started as a temporary favor, I took on the role seriously and applied my common-sense approach to the prison’s challenges. Our friendship and his trust were important in that chapter of my life.

Although it started as a temporary favor, I took on the role seriously and applied my common-sense approach to the prison’s challenges. Our friendship and his trust were important in that chapter of my life.

explicit_text: I was appointed warden of the Nevada State Prison by Governor Charlie Russell. The position was initially a temporary favor, but the prison’s problems proved to be an interesting challenge. My background as a miner and inspector helped me manage the facility with a practical, common-sense approach. It was a significant part of my career in Nevada.
